title: SKMCA Ajman secures Level 4 PSFHI accreditation in UAE | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: Evaluators examined how the hospital manages patient care across multiple departments, including safety protocols, infection control, medication handling, and continuous quality improvement processes.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
UAE—The Sheikh Khalifa Medical City—Ajman (SKMCA), overseen by The Medical Office under PureHealth, has secured Level 4 accreditation under the National Patient Safety Friendly Hospital Initiative (PSFHI).

The programme is led by the Ministry of Health and Prevention in collaboration with the World Health Organization, and it represents the highest recognition within its patient safety framework.

The accreditation followed a detailed assessment of SKMCA’s clinical operations, governance systems, and facility standards.

Evaluators examined how the hospital manages patient care across multiple departments, including safety protocols, infection control, medication handling, and continuous quality improvement processes.

Strong commitment to patient safety and clinical excellence

Level 4 status is awarded only to hospitals that demonstrate organisation-wide excellence in patient safety and healthcare delivery.

At SKMCA, the evaluation confirmed strong adherence to international standards, particularly those aligned with the PSFHI framework developed by the Ministry of Health and Prevention of the UAE, in partnership with WHO.

The hospital’s leadership emphasized that the achievement reflects long-term investment in building a safety-driven healthcare culture.

Systems for monitoring patient outcomes, reporting incidents, and improving clinical practices were highlighted as key strengths during the assessment.

Leadership response from The Medical Office and SKMCA

Dr. Aref Ali Abdulla Al Shehhi, CEO of The Medical Office, said the recognition reflects a sustained focus on global healthcare standards and patient-centred care across all facilities under its supervision.

He noted that patient safety remains a core pillar in the group’s healthcare strategy, supported by continuous training and quality assurance systems.

SKMCA CEO Mr. Khamis Al Mansoori also welcomed the accreditation, stating that it reflects the collective effort of healthcare professionals across departments.

He emphasized that the recognition confirms patients are receiving care aligned with internationally benchmarked safety and quality systems, supported by coordinated teamwork and clinical discipline.

PSFHI framework and evaluation standards

The PSFHI programme evaluates hospitals using strict criteria covering leadership commitment, clinical safety practices, patient rights, infection prevention and control, medication safety, and ongoing quality improvement.

Hospitals are required to demonstrate not only compliance but also consistency in applying safety measures across all levels of care.

Achieving Level 4 status indicates that SKMCA has met the highest threshold in the programme, reflecting maturity in its healthcare systems and operational consistency.

Continued focus on healthcare quality

SKMCA continues to strengthen its clinical governance systems and patient safety initiatives while aligning its operations with international healthcare benchmarks.

The institution remains part of broader healthcare transformation efforts led by PureHealth, which focuses on improving access, quality, and innovation in healthcare delivery across its network.
